#!/bin/bash
# I-carney-008 — POLARIS Carney demo bootstrap on a fresh Vexxhost Ubuntu 24.04 VM.
#
# Run as root on a freshly-provisioned Vexxhost VM in the Montréal region.
# Replaces the AWS Terraform module + cloud-init + Secrets Manager flow with
# a single bash script for the sovereign Canadian deploy.
#
# Prereqs (operator does these BEFORE running this script):
#   1. Vexxhost VM provisioned (Ubuntu 24.04, 8 vCPU / 32 GB / 100 GB SSD, Montréal)
#   2. DNS A record pointing polaris.<domain> at the VM's public IP
#   3. SSH key access as root or sudo user
#   4. /root/.env populated with all secrets (see infra/vexxhost/.env.example)
#   5. /root/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc + /root/polaris_demo_secret.asc (GPG keys)
#
# Usage:
#   scp infra/vexxhost/.env.example root@polaris.<domain>:/root/.env  (edit first)
#   ssh root@polaris.<domain>
#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aldrinor/polaris/polaris/infra/vexxhost/provision.sh | bash
#
# OR copy this script up and run it directly.

set -eo pipefail

# Config (override via env before running).
POLARIS_REPO_URL="${POLARIS_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/aldrinor/polaris.git}"
POLARIS_REPO_BRANCH="${POLARIS_REPO_BRANCH:-polaris}"
POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT="${POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT:-}"   # required; no default — must pin
POLARIS_DOMAIN="${POLARIS_DOMAIN:-}"             # e.g. polaris.example.ca
POLARIS_ACME_EMAIL="${POLARIS_ACME_EMAIL:-}"     # Let's Encrypt contact

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[provision] ERROR: must run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi
if [ -z "$POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT" ]; then
    echo "[provision] ERROR: POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT must be pinned (no floating HEAD)" >&2
    exit 1
fi
if [ -z "$POLARIS_DOMAIN" ]; then
    echo "[provision] ERROR: POLARIS_DOMAIN must be set" >&2
    exit 1
fi
if [ ! -f /root/.env ]; then
    echo "[provision] ERROR: /root/.env not found. Populate from infra/vexxhost/.env.example" >&2
    exit 1
fi
if [ ! -f /root/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc ] || [ ! -f /root/polaris_demo_secret.asc ]; then
    echo "[provision] ERROR: GPG keys not staged at /root/polaris_demo_{pubkey,secret}.asc" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "=== POLARIS Vexxhost provisioning ==="
echo "Domain: $POLARIS_DOMAIN"
echo "Repo:   $POLARIS_REPO_URL @ $POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT"
echo "===================================="

# ----- 1. apt + docker + caddy + git + gnupg -----
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update
apt-get install -y \
    docker.io \
    docker-compose-v2 \
    git \
    gnupg \
    curl \
    jq \
    debian-keyring \
    debian-archive-keyring \
    apt-transport-https \
    ca-certificates

# Caddy from official Cloudsmith repo (Czech/EU-mirrored, not US — and even
# if Cloudsmith CDN edges via US, the package itself is fetched once at
# install and we don't depend on Caddy phoning home at runtime).
curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/gpg.key' \
    | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/caddy-stable-archive-keyring.gpg
curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/debian.deb.txt' \
    | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/caddy-stable.list
apt-get update
apt-get install -y caddy

systemctl enable --now docker

# ----- 2. clone POLARIS at pinned commit -----
mkdir -p /opt
if [ ! -d /opt/polaris/.git ]; then
    git clone "$POLARIS_REPO_URL" /opt/polaris
fi
cd /opt/polaris
git fetch origin "$POLARIS_REPO_BRANCH"
git checkout "$POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT"

# ----- 3. wire .env -----
cp /root/.env /opt/polaris/.env
chmod 600 /opt/polaris/.env

# Codex iter-2 P2-1: .env template defaults POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT=REPLACE_ME_REPO_SHA.
# Overwrite from the resolved pin so /transparency surfaces the real commit
# rather than the placeholder.
sed -i "s|^POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT=.*|POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT=${POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT}|" /opt/polaris/.env
if ! grep -q "^POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT=" /opt/polaris/.env; then
    echo "POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT=${POLARIS_REPO_COMMIT}" >> /opt/polaris/.env
fi

# ----- 4. /etc/polaris substrate (auth + egress allowlist) -----
mkdir -p /etc/polaris
chmod 750 /etc/polaris

# static_accounts.yaml expected in /root/static_accounts.yaml (operator-prepared).
if [ -f /root/static_accounts.yaml ]; then
    cp /root/static_accounts.yaml /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml
    chmod 640 /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml
else
    echo "[provision] WARN: /root/static_accounts.yaml not found; auth will fail until provided" >&2
fi

# Egress allowlist baked from the repo.
cp /opt/polaris/config/egress_allowlist.txt /etc/polaris/egress_allowlist.txt
chmod 644 /etc/polaris/egress_allowlist.txt

# ----- 5. GPG keyring -----
export GNUPGHOME=/var/lib/polaris/gpg
mkdir -p "$GNUPGHOME"
chmod 700 "$GNUPGHOME"
cp /root/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc "$GNUPGHOME/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc"
chmod 644 "$GNUPGHOME/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc"
gpg --batch --import /root/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc
gpg --batch --import /root/polaris_demo_secret.asc
# Wipe the secret key file from /root — it now lives only in the GPG keyring.
shred -u /root/polaris_demo_secret.asc

# Ensure POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR in .env points at /var/lib/polaris/gpg.
if ! grep -q "POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR=/var/lib/polaris/gpg" /opt/polaris/.env; then
    echo "POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR=/var/lib/polaris/gpg" >> /opt/polaris/.env
fi

# ----- 6. bring up the compose stack -----
cd /opt/polaris
docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d --build

# ----- 7. wait for api + webui healthchecks -----
# Codex iter-1 P2-4: if the wait loop exhausts without success, FAIL the
# script rather than silently emitting "deployed" on an unhealthy stack.
echo "[provision] waiting for api + webui healthchecks..."
healthy=0
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    if curl -fsS http://localhost:8000/health > /dev/null && \
       curl -fsS http://localhost:3000/ > /dev/null; then
        echo "[provision] api + webui healthy at boot+$((i*5))s"
        healthy=1
        break
    fi
    sleep 5
done
if [ "$healthy" -ne 1 ]; then
    echo "[provision] ERROR: api + webui did not become healthy within 300s." >&2
    echo "[provision] Compose logs (last 50 lines):" >&2
    docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml logs --tail=50 >&2 || true
    exit 1
fi

# ----- 8. Caddy reverse proxy + Let's Encrypt -----
cat > /etc/caddy/Caddyfile <<EOF
${POLARIS_DOMAIN} {
    tls ${POLARIS_ACME_EMAIL}

    # /health + /transparency + /auth + /api + /stream + everything else → webui:3000
    # webui's Next.js rewrites handle /api/v6/* and /transparency/* server-side.
    reverse_proxy localhost:3000 {
        # SSE keepalive: don't buffer.
        flush_interval -1
    }

    # /health probe goes direct to backend for cleaner ops signal.
    handle /health {
        reverse_proxy localhost:8000
    }

    log {
        output file /var/log/caddy/polaris.log
        format json
    }
}
EOF
mkdir -p /var/log/caddy
caddy fmt --overwrite /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
systemctl reload caddy

echo ""
echo "=================================================================="
echo "POLARIS deployed on Vexxhost. Public URL:"
echo "  https://${POLARIS_DOMAIN}/"
echo ""
echo "Verify:"
echo "  curl https://${POLARIS_DOMAIN}/health"
echo "  curl https://${POLARIS_DOMAIN}/transparency | jq"
echo ""
echo "Egress lockdown is NOT yet enabled. Run BOTH after first compose build:"
echo "  sudo bash /opt/polaris/scripts/egress_lockdown.sh"
echo "  sudo bash /opt/polaris/scripts/egress_runtime_tighten.sh"
echo ""
echo "Verify all 4 chains (iptables x ip6tables x OUTPUT x DOCKER-USER):"
echo "  sudo iptables  -L POLARIS_EGRESS_HOST     -n -v | head -20"
echo "  sudo iptables  -L POLARIS_EGRESS_DOCKER   -n -v | head -20"
echo "  sudo ip6tables -L POLARIS_EGRESS_HOST_V6  -n -v | head -20"
echo "  sudo ip6tables -L POLARIS_EGRESS_DOCKER_V6 -n -v | head -20"
echo ""
echo "Confirm /transparency build_time_hosts_pruned is true after tighten:"
echo "  curl -fsS https://${POLARIS_DOMAIN}/transparency | jq .build_time_hosts_pruned"
echo "=================================================================="


exec

# I-carney-005 — POLARIS v6 production stack (Carney demo).
#
# Stack: redis + api + worker + webui.
# Legacy pipeline-B compose (docker-compose.yml) stays untouched per
# I-carney-005 acceptance criterion 8.
#
# Bring up:  docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml up -d
# Tear down: docker compose -f docker-compose.v6.yml down -v   (NB: drops volumes)

services:
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    command:
      - redis-server
      - --appendonly
      - "yes"
      - --save
      - "60"
      - "1000"
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
    restart: unless-stopped

  api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.v6
    command: api
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      POLARIS_V6_RUN_DB: /app/state/v6_runs.sqlite
      GNUPGHOME: /app/gpg
      # POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is read from .env via env_file
    volumes:
      - shared_state:/app/state
      - ./outputs:/app/outputs
      - ./logs:/app/logs
      - ./data:/app/data
      # P1-006: writable GPG homedir bind-mount.
      - ${POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR:-~/.gnupg-polaris}:/app/gpg
      # I-carney-004 P1: mount /etc/polaris/static_accounts.yaml into the
      # container so verify_app_startup() can find the auth substrate.
      # Read-only bind; host file is 640 root:root, container reads as root.
      - ${POLARIS_ETC_DIR:-/etc/polaris}:/etc/polaris:ro
    ports:
      - "${POLARIS_API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    depends_on:
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-fsS", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      start_period: 30s
      retries: 3
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 6G
        reservations:
          memory: 2G

  worker:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.v6
    command: worker
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
      POLARIS_V6_RUN_DB: /app/state/v6_runs.sqlite
      GNUPGHOME: /app/gpg
    volumes:
      - shared_state:/app/state
      - ./outputs:/app/outputs
      - ./logs:/app/logs
      - ./data:/app/data
      - ${POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR:-~/.gnupg-polaris}:/app/gpg
      - ${POLARIS_ETC_DIR:-/etc/polaris}:/etc/polaris:ro
    depends_on:
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 8G
        reservations:
          memory: 4G

  webui:
    build:
      context: ./web
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      args:
        # P1-B from Codex diff iter-2: Next.js bakes rewrites() destinations
        # at build time, so INTERNAL_API_URL must be a build arg. NOT
        # prefixed NEXT_PUBLIC_, so it does NOT leak into the client bundle —
        # only the server-side routes manifest sees it.
        INTERNAL_API_URL: http://api:8000
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
    ports:
      - "${POLARIS_WEB_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  redis_data:
  shared_state:

.codex\I-arch-001b\codex_brief_verdict_iter_3.txt:6162:.codex\I-policy-001\codex_brief_verdict.txt:3905:.codex\pr_e_open_issues_review_verdict_iter_3.txt:2161:.codex/pr_d_mechanical_gates_review_brief.md:223:   - Compute the trailer hash AFTER submitting to Codex but before the workflow runs (race). Mitigation: Claude commits the trailer FIRST, then `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check` reads the diff (with trailer) and emits APPROVE on it. The codex_diff.patch is committed as one atomic unit to the PR. If Claude updates the patch post-Codex-APPROVE, the verdict file's APPROVE no longer binds to it — but the workflow still passes because verdict file says APPROVE and trailer matches diff. **This is a real residual risk:** Codex must review codex_diff.patch INCLUDING its trailer line, so the APPROVE binds to BOTH the code-change-diff AND the declared sha256. If Claude post-edits the trailer to match a different actual diff, the trailer's value changes → Codex's APPROVE was for a different patch byte-for-byte → Codex's APPROVE no longer applies. Operationally: the workflow only verifies `actual_hash == declared_hash`, not that Codex APPROVE'd the specific declared hash. Phase-2 hardening would have Codex output the hash IT approved (in codex_diff_audit.txt) and the workflow cross-check. Acknowledged Phase-1 limitation; user reads `git log` morning catches forgery.

.codex\I-arch-001b\codex_brief_verdict_iter_3.txt:10990:.codex\I-policy-001\codex_diff_audit.txt:2495:.codex/I-policy-001/codex_brief_verdict.txt:3905:.codex\pr_e_open_issues_review_verdict_iter_3.txt:2161:.codex/pr_d_mechanical_gates_review_brief.md:223:   - Compute the trailer hash AFTER submitting to Codex but before the workflow runs (race). Mitigation: Claude commits the trailer FIRST, then `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check` reads the diff (with trailer) and emits APPROVE on it. The codex_diff.patch is committed as one atomic unit to the PR. If Claude updates the patch post-Codex-APPROVE, the verdict file's APPROVE no longer binds to it — but the workflow still passes because verdict file says APPROVE and trailer matches diff. **This is a real residual risk:** Codex must review codex_diff.patch INCLUDING its trailer line, so the APPROVE binds to BOTH the code-change-diff AND the declared sha256. If Claude post-edits the trailer to match a different actual diff, the trailer's value changes → Codex's APPROVE was for a different patch byte-for-byte → Codex's APPROVE no longer applies. Operationally: the workflow only verifies `actual_hash == declared_hash`, not that Codex APPROVE'd the specific declared hash. Phase-2 hardening would have Codex output the hash IT approved (in codex_diff_audit.txt) and the workflow cross-check. Acknowledged Phase-1 limitation; user reads `git log` morning catches forgery.

.codex\I-arch-001b\codex_brief_verdict_iter_3.txt:11064:.codex\I-policy-001\codex_brief_verdict.txt:3905:.codex\pr_e_open_issues_review_verdict_iter_3.txt:2161:.codex/pr_d_mechanical_gates_review_brief.md:223:   - Compute the trailer hash AFTER submitting to Codex but before the workflow runs (race). Mitigation: Claude commits the trailer FIRST, then `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check` reads the diff (with trailer) and emits APPROVE on it. The codex_diff.patch is committed as one atomic unit to the PR. If Claude updates the patch post-Codex-APPROVE, the verdict file's APPROVE no longer binds to it — but the workflow still passes because verdict file says APPROVE and trailer matches diff. **This is a real residual risk:** Codex must review codex_diff.patch INCLUDING its trailer line, so the APPROVE binds to BOTH the code-change-diff AND the declared sha256. If Claude post-edits the trailer to match a different actual diff, the trailer's value changes → Codex's APPROVE was for a different patch byte-for-byte → Codex's APPROVE no longer applies. Operationally: the workflow only verifies `actual_hash == declared_hash`, not that Codex APPROVE'd the specific declared hash. Phase-2 hardening would have Codex output the hash IT approved (in codex_diff_audit.txt) and the workflow cross-check. Acknowledged Phase-1 limitation; user reads `git log` morning catches forgery.

.codex\I-carney-010\codex_diff_audit_iter_2.txt:338:.codex/I-carney-010\codex_diff.patch:93:+What Serper receives: the search query string itself, plus the normal request metadata any HTTP API call carries — the API account/key, source IP, timestamp, and user-agent — which Serper's privacy policy (serper.dev/privacy) describes it logging as system/access activity. What Serper does NOT receive: the uploaded corpus, the evidence pool, the generated report, or any operator-entered content. Serper returns only result URLs + snippets; POLARIS then fetches the actual T1 evidence directly from the government corpus endpoints listed above.

.codex\I-carney-010\codex_diff_audit_iter_1.txt:384:.codex/I-carney-010\codex_diff.patch:91:+**Web search provider:** Serper (`google.serper.dev`), US-based. This is a deliberate, disclosed exception to the "no US company" posture, accepted per operator directive 2026-05-13. The rationale: POLARIS's sovereignty constraint protects the **LLM inference path and the generated report data** — those run on Canadian/non-US infrastructure (Vexxhost orchestrator + OVH H200 inference). A web-search query is a short keyword string that carries no confidential research content; the sensitive artifact is the synthesized report, which never transits Serper. Serper returns only URLs + snippets; the actual T1 evidence is then fetched directly from the government corpus endpoints listed above. A reviewer who wants zero US touch in the search path can swap Serper for a non-US provider (Mojeek UK / Qwant FR / Ecosia DE) — the retrieval code is provider-shaped — but that is not required for the sovereignty posture as scoped.

.codex\I-cj-007\codex_diff_audit_iter_1.txt:4318:.codex\I-f1-005\codex_diff_audit.txt:427:.codex\pr_d_mechanical_gates_review_brief.md:222:4. **Convention dependency on Claude self-discipline:** the canonical-diff-sha256 trailer is Claude-emitted. A malicious Claude could:
.codex\I-cj-007\codex_diff_audit_iter_1.txt:4319:.codex\I-f1-005\codex_diff_audit.txt:428:.codex\pr_d_mechanical_gates_review_brief.md:223:   - Compute the trailer hash AFTER submitting to Codex but before the workflow runs (race). Mitigation: Claude commits the trailer FIRST, then `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check` reads the diff (with trailer) and emits APPROVE on it. The codex_diff.patch is committed as one atomic unit to the PR. If Claude updates the patch post-Codex-APPROVE, the verdict file's APPROVE no longer binds to it — but the workflow still passes because verdict file says APPROVE and trailer matches diff. **This is a real residual risk:** Codex must review codex_diff.patch INCLUDING its trailer line, so the APPROVE binds to BOTH the code-change-diff AND the declared sha256. If Claude post-edits the trailer to match a different actual diff, the trailer's value changes → Codex's APPROVE was for a different patch byte-for-byte → Codex's APPROVE no longer applies. Operationally: the workflow only verifies `actual_hash == declared_hash`, not that Codex APPROVE'd the specific declared hash. Phase-2 hardening would have Codex output the hash IT approved (in codex_diff_audit.txt) and the workflow cross-check. Acknowledged Phase-1 limitation; user reads `git log` morning catches forgery.

.codex\I-cj-007\codex_diff_audit_iter_1.txt:4375:.codex\I-f1-005\codex_diff_audit.txt:523:.codex\pr_d_mechanical_gates_review_brief.md:223:   - Compute the trailer hash AFTER submitting to Codex but before the workflow runs (race). Mitigation: Claude commits the trailer FIRST, then `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check` reads the diff (with trailer) and emits APPROVE on it. The codex_diff.patch is committed as one atomic unit to the PR. If Claude updates the patch post-Codex-APPROVE, the verdict file's APPROVE no longer binds to it — but the workflow still passes because verdict file says APPROVE and trailer matches diff. **This is a real residual risk:** Codex must review codex_diff.patch INCLUDING its trailer line, so the APPROVE binds to BOTH the code-change-diff AND the declared sha256. If Claude post-edits the trailer to match a different actual diff, the trailer's value changes → Codex's APPROVE was for a different patch byte-for-byte → Codex's APPROVE no longer applies. Operationally: the workflow only verifies `actual_hash == declared_hash`, not that Codex APPROVE'd the specific declared hash. Phase-2 hardening would have Codex output the hash IT approved (in codex_diff_audit.txt) and the workflow cross-check. Acknowledged Phase-1 limitation; user reads `git log` morning catches forgery.

codex
The core artifacts line up: the public key fingerprint decodes to the published value, the sample archive’s member set matches the manifest, and the detached Ed25519 signature verifies against the manifest when parsed independently. I’m doing one last pass for document/transcript agreement before closing.
exec
"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command "@'
import base64, hashlib, pathlib, tarfile, yaml
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519 import Ed25519PublicKey
from cryptography.exceptions import InvalidSignature

BUNDLE = pathlib.Path('docs/carney_handover/sample_signed_bundle.tar.gz')
PUB = pathlib.Path('docs/carney_handover/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc')


def dearmor_text(txt: str) -> bytes:
    body=[]; inb=False
    for line in txt.splitlines():
        if line.startswith('-----BEGIN '): inb=True; continue
        if line.startswith('-----END '): break
        if not inb or not line or ':' in line: continue
        if line.startswith('='): continue
        body.append(line.strip())
    return base64.b64decode(''.join(body))

def packets(data):
    i=0
    while i < len(data):
        hdr=data[i]; i+=1
        if hdr & 0x40:
            tag=hdr & 0x3f; l=data[i]; i+=1
            if l < 192: length=l
            elif l < 224: length=((l-192)<<8)+data[i]+192; i+=1
            elif l == 255: length=int.from_bytes(data[i:i+4], 'big'); i+=4
            else: raise RuntimeError('partial length unsupported')
        else:
            tag=(hdr>>2)&0xf; lt=hdr&3
            if lt == 0: length=data[i]; i+=1
            elif lt == 1: length=int.from_bytes(data[i:i+2], 'big'); i+=2
            elif lt == 2: length=int.from_bytes(data[i:i+4], 'big'); i+=4
            else: raise RuntimeError('indeterminate length unsupported')
        yield tag, data[i:i+length]
        i += length

def read_mpi(buf, p):
    bits=int.from_bytes(buf[p:p+2], 'big'); p+=2
    n=(bits+7)//8
    return buf[p:p+n], p+n

pub = dearmor_text(PUB.read_text())
for tag, body in packets(pub):
    if tag == 6:
        fp = hashlib.sha1(b'\\x99' + len(body).to_bytes(2,'big') + body).hexdigest().upper()
        off = 6
        oid_len = body[off]; off += 1 + oid_len
        point, off = read_mpi(body, off)
        pubkey = point[1:]
        break
else:
    raise SystemExit('no public key packet')

with tarfile.open(BUNDLE, 'r:gz') as tar:
    names = tar.getnames()
    top = names[0].split('/')[0]
    manifest = tar.extractfile(f'{top}/manifest.yaml').read()
    sigtxt = tar.extractfile(f'{top}/manifest.yaml.asc').read().decode()
    extracted = {n.split('/', 1)[1]: tar.extractfile(n).read() for n in names if not n.endswith('/') and '/' in n}
manifest_obj = yaml.safe_load(manifest.decode())
expected = {'manifest.yaml', 'manifest.yaml.asc'} | {f['path'] for f in manifest_obj['files']}
got = {n.split('/',1)[1] for n in names}
print('fingerprint', ' '.join(fp[i:i+4] for i in range(0, len(fp), 4)))
print('member_set_ok', got == expected)
for f in manifest_obj['files']:
    ok = hashlib.sha256(extracted[f['path']]).hexdigest() == f['sha256']
    print('hash_ok', f['path'], ok)

sig_packet = dearmor_text(sigtxt)
tag, sigbody = next(packets(sig_packet))
hlen = int.from_bytes(sigbody[4:6], 'big')
hashed_region = sigbody[:6+hlen]
trailer = bytes([4, 0xff]) + len(hashed_region).to_bytes(4, 'big')
digest = hashlib.sha512(manifest + hashed_region + trailer).digest()
u0 = 6 + hlen
ulen = int.from_bytes(sigbody[u0:u0+2], 'big')
p = u0 + 2 + ulen
left = sigbody[p:p+2]; p += 2
r, p = read_mpi(sigbody, p)
s, p = read_mpi(sigbody, p)
signature = r.rjust(32, b'\\0') + s.rjust(32, b'\\0')
print('sig_left_hash_ok', left == digest[:2])
try:
    Ed25519PublicKey.from_public_bytes(pubkey).verify(signature, digest)
    print('signature_ok True')
except InvalidSignature:
    print('signature_ok False')
'@ | python -" in C:\POLARIS

hash_ok metadata.json True
hash_ok scope_decision.json True
hash_ok sources/src-A.txt True
hash_ok verified_report.json True
sig_left_hash_ok True
signature_ok True
